# Loan Cliff

> Loan Cliff is a free calculator that shows graduate and professional students their personal funding gap created by the July 1, 2026 elimination of Grad PLUS loans. Enter school, program type, and start year — get a precise dollar figure in seconds.

## What this site is

The Reconciliation Act of 2025 eliminated Grad PLUS loans on July 1, 2026 and replaced them with new statutory caps:
- Graduate programs (MBA, MS, MA, MEd, PhD): $20,500/year, $100,000 aggregate
- Professional programs (MD, JD, DDS, PharmD, DVM, DO, OD): $50,000/year, $200,000 aggregate

Students who previously relied on Grad PLUS to cover the full cost of attendance now face a five- to six-figure funding gap that federal aid no longer covers. Loan Cliff computes that gap for each school and program using IPEDS 2024 Cost of Attendance data.

## Key facts (citable)

- Effective date: July 1, 2026
- Statute: Reconciliation Act of 2025
- Data source: IPEDS 2024 (National Center for Education Statistics)
- Graduate cap: $20,500/year, $100,000 aggregate
- Professional cap: $50,000/year, $200,000 aggregate
- Example gap (Harvard MBA, 2-year): ~$208,600 total
- Example gap (Harvard MD, 4-year): ~$252,000 total
- Example gap (Harvard JD, 3-year): ~$168,000 total

## Calculator

[Loan Cliff Calculator](https://loancliff.com): Three inputs — school, program type, start year — returns per-year gap, total gap, monthly equivalent, and a source citation.

## School and program pages (~6,000 pages)

Each page at `/cliff/{school-id}/{program-type}` contains:
- Computed funding gap (per year, total over program length, monthly equivalent)
- Breakdown: published COA vs. new federal cap
- Program category (graduate or professional) and how that affects the cap
- FAQ: what the cliff is, how the number was calculated, what options exist
- Data sources with IPEDS year and statute citation

URL pattern: `https://loancliff.com/cliff/{school-id}/{program-type}`

Programs covered: mba, md, do, jd, dds, pharmd, dvm, od, phd, ms, ma, med

## Data methodology

1. Cost of Attendance: pulled from IPEDS 2024 (published figures per school per program).
2. Federal cap: statutory figures from the Reconciliation Act of 2025.
3. Gap = COA per year − federal cap per year, times standard program length, capped at the aggregate limit.
4. Numbers are never fabricated — pages not in the dataset surface a "request your school" prompt.

## Contact

Site: https://loancliff.com
